title: Queues, Streams, Super Streams, Retention, and Replay · Compiler Case: Workload Selection Evidence
summary: A high-throughput replay workload uses one quorum queue with a ten-million-message backlog, while another service selects streams but expects destructive consumption and ordinary queue client locality. Planned artifact: Add and verify the workload selection evidence increment in queue-versus-stream workload decision and replay plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select classic queue, quorum queue, stream, super stream, or external log using loss, latency, throughput, backlog, replay, routing, and operational evidence.
Account for stream protocol, replica locality, leader and follower reads, connection routing, confirms, and failure recovery.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Workload Selection Evidence · Theory Workload Selection Evidence
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 8012 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to choose queue, stream, or super stream from replay, backlog, throughput, and order-key evidence; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then fail the canary and prove older consumers remain compatible during rollback. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Workload Selection Evidence contract for the supplied data.
